title: Villa Almerico Capra (La Rotonda) plan, from I quattro libri dell'architettura
creator: Andrea Palladio (delineator); plate from I quattro libri dell'architettura
rightsholder_or_source: Metropolitan Museum of Art (Open Access); via Wikimedia Commons
research_notes: Combined sheet: plan plus elevation/section on one plate (the plan is the lower portion). High-resolution MET Open Access scan. Late-Renaissance graphic convention (no dimensions).
research_features: Perfectly symmetric central round hall (rotunda) inscribed in a square, with four identical projecting temple-front porches/stairs on all sides. Excellent test of radial/curved geometry and 4-fold symmetry.
feature_highlights: round central hall (rotunda) inscribed in a square block
four identical projecting temple-front porticoes with steps
rooms tagged with inscribed dimension figures (P. 30, P. 26, P. 15, P. 12)
combined plan plus elevation/section on one Renaissance plate
straight-walled perimeter rooms around a single curved space
supported_examples: straight walls at angles forming rectangular perimeter rooms
symmetric room layout with paired mirror-image rooms
linear inscribed dimension figures on rooms
raster underlay with scale calibration to trace the historic plate
roadmap_examples: site/textual metadata for a named historic villa

gap_features.why: the great central rotunda is a true round room bounded by curved arc walls, not straight segments
the four projecting temple-front porticoes are roofed, columned, open-air loggias rather than fully wall-enclosed rooms
